articles.title: Francis Ngannou: “Keep the Legacy, I’ll Take the Money”
articles.description: Francis Ngannou made an interesting statement during a recent MVP press conference: "Keep the legacy… I’ll take the money." For Chael Sonnen, it was one of the most refreshing comments he’s heard in combat sports. Fighters spend years talking about their legacy, but Chael argues that legacy is something no fighter can actually control. It’s simply what people say about you long after your career is over. Ngannou’s point wasn’t that he literally doesn’t care — it’s that he understands legacy isn’t something you can manage. And when you look at Francis’ career, the idea that he avoids difficult fights doesn’t hold up. Ngannou has already fought some of the toughest names in combat sports: * Cain Velasquez * Stipe Miocic (twice) * Tyson Fury * Anthony Joshua He even agreed to fights that never happened — including a potential matchup with Rico Verhoeven. Chael explains why Ngannou’s attitude toward legacy may actually be the most honest approach a fighter can take. Because legacy isn’t an opponent. And it’s not something you can fight. articles.title: Chael Addresses Dan Hooker Allegations
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 50% Off with code CHAEL at https://takeultra.com ! #UltraPouches #adBad Dan Hooker recently found himself in the middle of a rumor that had nothing to do with fighting. A story resurfaced involving a past personal relationship, and suddenly the MMA world was talking about gossip instead of fights. Hooker made the unusual decision to address it directly. According to Hooker, the situation involved a consensual arrangement with an adult, and the person involved later chose to speak publicly after the relationship ended. Chael believes Hooker handled the situation the right way — by telling the truth and addressing it head-on. But Chael also explains why stories like this can be dangerous for fighters, and why sometimes the smartest PR move is to say one final thing: "This is the last time I’m going to speak about this." Chael also reflects on a similar situation involving Brock Lesnar, and why letting time pass can sometimes be the only way for controversies like this to disappear. At the end of the day, Chael says the focus should be on what Dan Hooker is actually known for — fighting in the Octagon and continuing his climb toward a title opportunity. Because if we’re going to talk about Dan Hooker…it should probably be about fighting. articles.title: Why the UFC Chose Strickland vs Chimaev (And What Happens to Imavov?)
articles.description: Sean Strickland vs Khamzat Chimaev is officially happening… but that raises a much bigger question. What happened to Nassourdine Imavov? For months the assumption inside the MMA world was that Imavov was next in line for the title shot at middleweight. He beat top contenders, nobody was calling him out, and the whispers inside the locker room suggested the fight was already locked in. Then suddenly the UFC announces Strickland vs Chimaev. articles.title: It’s Time to Bring Back Tsarukyan & Mokaev to the Octagon
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan vs Muhammad Mokaev wasn’t just a grappling match — it was a statement. Two of the best young fighters in the sport found a way to keep their names relevant without fighting in the UFC, which is incredibly difficult to do. Muhammad Mokaev was 14-0 and ranked #3 in the world when the UFC released him. The promotion said he was “difficult to deal with,” something Mokaev says caught him completely off guard. Instead of disappearing, Mokaev has done the opposite. He’s taken wrestling matches in Russia, fights in other promotions, and now stepped in on short notice to grapple Arman Tsarukyan — giving up nearly 30 pounds in the process. Tsarukyan deserves credit too. Taking a match where the public believes you're supposed to win is never easy. If you win, you get no credit. If you lose, you hear about it forever. The result was a fast, chaotic grappling match before Tsarukyan eventually locked up the choke. But the real story isn’t who won. The story is that Mokaev is still competing everywhere he can — and Chael believes that deserves recognition. Sometimes the hardest thing in combat sports isn’t winning fights. It’s staying relevant when you’re not fighting in the UFC. And right now, Mokaev is doing everything he can to prove he belongs back. articles.title: The Night Francis Shocked the World #ufc
articles.description: I’m telling you flat out — that was one of the most remarkable sporting moments I’ve ever witnessed. Francis Ngannou stepped into a boxing ring with the lineal heavyweight king, Tyson Fury, and he didn’t just survive… he won. At least, he should have. One licensed judge scored it for Francis. A whole lot of fans scored it for Francis. Even Joe Rogan said he thought Francis won. And when that knockdown happened in Round 2? I jumped off the couch. That wasn’t luck. That wasn’t a gimmick. That was power, timing, and a fighter who refused to play the role of opponent. Let’s be clear about something: Francis wasn’t a novelty act crossing over. He was a former undisputed UFC heavyweight champion proving he belonged on the biggest stage in combat sports. So what’s the real question? Do you want to be “world champion”… Or do you want the biggest fights, the biggest moments, and the biggest paydays? Because Francis walked into enemy territory, shocked the world, and left with global respect. He changed how MMA fighters are viewed in boxing — overnight. That’s bigger than a belt. That’s legacy. And deep down, we all know what we saw. Watch it back. Tell me he didn’t win. #FrancisNgannou #TysonFury #Boxing #MMA #UFC #CombatSports #Heavyweight #FightNight #BoxingNews

articles.title: If Pereira Wins the Interim Belt… Does It Count?
articles.description: If Pereira Wins the Interim Belt… Does It Count? Is He Really Champ Champ Champ? If Alex Pereira wins at the UFC’s massive White House event, could he actually become champ-champ-champ? Chael Sonnen breaks down one of the most controversial topics in MMA: interim championship belts. Most fighters dismiss interim titles entirely. Some champions have even thrown the belt on the ground after winning it. But Chael argues something very different. History shows that interim belts often become undisputed belts. In fact, that exact scenario recently happened in the UFC heavyweight division when Tom Aspinall was elevated from interim champion to undisputed champion. So what happens if Pereira wins the interim heavyweight championship while already holding titles in other divisions? Does that count as three belts at once? Many fighters — including Conor McGregor and Henry Cejudo — don’t see interim belts the same way Chael does. But the UFC continues to create them for one reason: Because sometimes the interim champion becomes the real champion. articles.title: Fact: Stars Are No Longer Needed in UFC...
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 50% Off with code CHAEL at takeultra.com! #UltraPouches #adbad Jake Paul recently claimed that the UFC is dying. But Chael Sonnen says the reality is much more complicated. With the UFC securing an $8 billion media rights deal, the company is entering a completely new era — one where the traditional star-driven pay-per-view model may no longer apply. For decades, combat sports revolved around a few massive draws like Conor McGregor, Jon Jones, Tito Ortiz, and Randy Couture. These fighters carried entire events and were responsible for generating the majority of revenue. But the sport may be shifting into something entirely different. Instead of needing superstar fighters to sell pay-per-views, the UFC may now be operating on a numbered event model where the promotion itself — not the fighters — is the main attraction. Chael explains why this shift could completely change how fighters build careers, how promotions structure events, and why even the biggest stars in the sport may no longer be required to headline every card. Meanwhile, Jake Paul’s MVP promotion is experimenting with a different strategy altogether — potentially building fights around crossover athletes like Francis Ngannou and even future boxing matchups with Jake Paul himself. So is the UFC dying? articles.title: Ronda Rousey Was SAVAGE at the Press Conference
articles.description: Ronda Rousey showed up to the press conference and did exactly what she was supposed to do. And Chael Sonnen loved every second of it. During the heated media event promoting the massive Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano fight, Rousey didn’t hold back. She fired shots at the UFC, called out Valentina Shevchenko, and even took direct jabs at her opponent sitting right next to her. It was raw. It was uncomfortable. It was classic Ronda. But according to Chael, that’s exactly how it should be. If you’re part of a promotion, you pick a side and back your team. Rousey came representing Jake Paul’s MVP promotion, and she made it crystal clear she wasn’t there to praise the UFC. Still, Chael pushes back on one major claim Ronda made: that the UFC is the worst place for fighters to compete. Chael explains why the reality of the fight business may actually prove the opposite. From fighter pay to contracts, bonuses, and the structure of the sport, the UFC may still be the most stable and reliable place in all of combat sports. And while Ronda may have been cutting a promo, she definitely succeeded in doing one thing: Getting everyone talking again. #RondaRousey #UFC #ChaelSonnen #MMA #GinaCarano #DanaWhite 0:00 Ronda Rousey’s fiery press conference 0:15 Why Chael loved Ronda’s attitude 0:45 The promo against Valentina Shevchenko 1:20 Ronda’s comments about Gina Carano 2:45 Is the UFC really the worst place to fight? 3:10 What other MMA promotions exist today 4:00 Why MVP is playing a different game 5:10 Chael’s problem with the criticism of the UFC 6:00 Dana White’s impact on fighter contracts 7:15 The truth about fighter pay and bonuses 8:10 Why Ronda’s promo worked perfectly Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Hard Truth About Jon Jones’ Future #ufc
articles.description: This might be the strangest position we’ve ever seen Jon Jones in. He’s 38. He’s still elite. But now he’s talking about arthritis. Hip issues. Physical limits. And suddenly, the conversation changes. It’s no longer about dominance. It’s about durability. For a guy who’s spent his entire career making the impossible look easy, this is a hard reality to watch. You can hear it in his tone. You can see it in the situation. Time is undefeated — even for legends. Behind the scenes, there were negotiations. There were asks. There were plans. But the division kept moving. A fighter Jones finished in minutes got a major opportunity. The belt picture shifted. And now we’re back to the scenario that was supposed to happen months ago: If Jon comes back, he likely has to fight for a belt. If he wins it, he defends it against Tom Aspinall. Full circle. It’s not glamorous. It’s not perfect. But it’s probably the cleanest solution. The question isn’t whether Jon still has greatness. The question is whether his body will let him chase it. And that’s a brutally human place for an all-time great to be. Does he push through one more run? Or protect what’s left and walk away? Legacy vs longevity. Greatness vs reality. Not an easy call — even for Jon Jones. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #TomAspinall #Heavyweight #UFCTitle #FightNews #CombatSports #GOATDebate

articles.title: Islam Makhachev vs Topuria BEEF EXPLODES! WHITE HOUSE CARD DRAMA! | BISPING REACTS
articles.description: 'HE CHICKENED OUT!?" Ilia Topuria has gone on a fiery Twitter rant accusing Islam Makhachev of avoiding a fight with him at the upcoming UFC White House event. According to Topuria, the fight was originally discussed for the historic card before Makhachev allegedly pulled out due to injury, leading to Justin Gaethje stepping in for the main event instead. Makhachev quickly fired back online, denying the accusations and claiming Topuria is the one hiding behind insults. With the two trading shots publicly, the rivalry between them may be far from over. In the meantime, Topuria is set to headline the White House card against Justin Gaethje — a fight that could add another massive knockout to his incredible run through the division. articles.title: Ronda Rousey Says Paramount Deal Screwed the Carano Fight
articles.description: Ronda Rousey said they originally had a great deal with Dana White for the Carano fight on December show but once the fight got pushed into 2026, the Paramount+ deal nixed it. "We were going to do a New Year's and it was going to be under the last fight under the pay -per -view model and he offered me the best pay -per -view structure ever and I was so grateful but then you know Gina said she needed more time to get in the best shape possible... And once they moved it to the streaming model it's just not about putting on the best fights possible. You know anymore."
